title: erigeron macdonaldii
intro: Erigeron macdonaldii, also known as the pale inflorescent fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Erigeron macdonaldii include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion. Erigeron macdonaldii can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, often combined with other herbs, to be used in traditional herbal medicine.
